Welcome to Habitline. By using our app or website, you agree to the following Terms & Conditions. Please read them carefully.
By accessing or using Habitline, you agree to these Terms.If you do not agree, please stop using the app or website.
Habitline is designed to help users track habits, routines, and personal goals.You agree to:
Use the app legally and responsibly
Not misuse or interfere with the service
Do not attempt to copy, reverse engineer, or exploit the app’s code or design
When you create an account (if applicable), you must:
Contact us immediately if you suspect unauthorized access
We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate our policies.
Your privacy is important to us.All data collection and usage is described in our Privacy Policy.
By using Habitline, you consent to the processing of your data as described there.
Habitline’s brand, design, content, graphics, and code are owned by Habitline.
Sell, rent, or distribute our materials
We aim to keep Habitline available at all times but cannot guarantee:
We may update or discontinue features at any time.
Habitline may offer AI-based habit recommendations.These suggestions:
This is not medical or professional advice
Should be used at your own discretion
Habitline is provided “as is.”We are not responsible for:
Any outcome related to how you use the app
Your use of the app is at your own risk.
We may integrate with third-party tools (analytics, hosting, crash reporting).These services have their own terms and privacy policies.
We may update these Terms occasionally.Revised versions will be posted on this page with a new “Last updated” date.
If you have questions about these Terms & Conditions, Contact us.

Habitline is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use our app and website.
Name and email (when you contact support or subscribe)
Habit details you choose to add
Preferences and settings within the app
We collect basic usage data to improve performance:
We do not collect sensitive personal information.
Run core features (habit tracking, reminders, AI suggestions)
Send important updates about the app
All data is stored securely on encrypted servers.
Only essential team members may access anonymized analytics.
We only share data in these limited cases:
For cloud hosting, analytics, or crash reporting — all under strict privacy agreements.
If required by law, we may share necessary information.
We do not share your personal or habit data with advertisers.
Request a copy of your stored data
Email us at support@habitline.app for any of the above.
We keep your information only as long as necessary to provide the app’s functionality.If you delete your account or send a request, your data will be permanently removed.
Habitline is not intended for children under 13.We do not knowingly collect data from children.
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Updates will be posted on this page with a new “Last updated” date.
For questions about this Privacy Policy or your data, contact us
No spam. Just simple advice for staying consistent.

/changelog.title: Habitline - Health & Wellness Framer Template
/changelog.description: Launch your health or wellness app faster with this modern Framer template designed for startups and digital health platforms.
p: Explore the latest features, performance updates, and AI enhancements.
•  AI Routine Builder — Generates full routines based on your goals (morning, focus, evening).
•  Added Dark Mode for better readability and night-time use.
•  Introduced Shared Routines so you can send or follow routines from friends or coaches.
•  Dashboard and weekly overview now load twice as fast.
•  Smoother syncing between habits, reminders, and streak counters.
•  Better accuracy in AI timing suggestions.
•  Patched minor issues in weekly analytics export.
•  Fixed reminder delays for habits with custom schedules.
•  Added AI Habit Recommendations — Suggests better times and routines based on your behavior.
•  Introduced Custom Routine Rules so you can set flexible streaks and exceptions that match real life.
•  Simplified onboarding with a clearer routine setup guide.
•  Faster syncing across devices for habit updates and streak progress.
•  Better accuracy in AI-driven decision branching.
•  Fixed occasional delay when loading the Today screen.
•  Resolved a bug causing duplicate check-ins for repeated habits.
No spam. Just simple advice for staying consistent.

/about.title: Habitline - Health & Wellness Framer Template
/about.description: Launch your health or wellness app faster with this modern Framer template designed for startups and digital health platforms.
h1: A simpler way to build habits
h2: Most habit tools focus on streaks, pressure, and unrealistic expectations. Our mission is to create a system that works with real human behavior.
The people who drive the work
Build better habits with less effort
h3: Be part of shaping systems that keep teams aligned and productive.
Build better habits with less effort
Scan the QR code to download the app
p: Your habits shift as your day shifts, and the app supports those changes instead of punishing them. We focus on small wins, gentle nudges, and simple structure so people can stay consistent even when life gets messy.
A clear list, helpful reminders, and easy tracking.
Adapts to your changing day so routines stay realistic.
Focuses on small, doable actions that build real habits.
Encourages consistency with gentle, supportive nudges.
Most tools expect perfect streaks, fixed schedules, and unlimited motivation. But real life isn’t that consistent. Days get busy. Plans shift. People forget.
Users stay on track most of the month.
Regular habits are completed each month.
People are using Habitline around the world.
Track what matters, stay organized, and improve at your own pace.
No spam. Just simple advice for staying consistent.

p: A calmer way to build habits
You see the right habits at the right time so your day never feels crowded.
Used by people to improve routines.
Stay consistent with a system that fits into real life. Simple cards, clear routines, and gentle nudges help you build progress that lasts.
Habitline brings clarity to your routines with clean cards, realistic progress tracking, and guidance that adapts to your day.
Traditional habit trackers are too rigid. Miss one day and your 50-day streak is gone forever.
A simple view that shows only the habits that match your current time of day.
Group habits into simple blocks so your day feels organized instead of scattered.
*Simple blocks help you stay on track without thinking.
A clear summary of your week that highlights what improved and what needs adjusting.
Short, calm nudges that help you stay consistent without pushing too hard.
Time to stretch and refresh before your next task.
A quick reset helps reduce stiffness.
Stay on track for the next hour.
Your focus session is almost done.
Tidy up and prepare for tomorrow.
Protect your focus for what matters.
Sit tall and realign your body.
For anyone handling long workdays, shifting priorities, and tight deadlines.
And for every kind of daily rhythm
Stopped breaking habits on weekends after switching to Habitline
Logged 40 focus sessions this month with Routine Stacks
Finally keeps his day organized with routine-based habit groups
Habitline learns your patterns and offers small, useful suggestions that help you stay consistent without guessing what to do next.
Suggests the best time to remind you.
Highlights what’s working and what’s slipping.
Helps you recover when you miss a day.
Habitline made my mornings feel manageable again.
The weekly insights are what sold me. They show exactly where I fall off and help me adjust without feeling guilty or overwhelmed.
This is the first habit app that doesn’t overwhelm me. Everything feels calm, structured, and intentional.
I used to ignore reminders from other apps, but these feel calm and well-timed. It’s like the app knows when I’m actually able to do something.
Focus blocks changed the way I work. I get more done in two hours now than what used to take half a day.
The simple visuals and progress cues make it easy to stay consistent every day.
It’s the first habit app that doesn’t overwhelm me.
I actually stick to my routines now. Small steps finally add up
Feels tailored to my day and keeps me motivated.
You can track as many as you want, but most users keep 5–8 habits for better consistency.
Track what matters, stay organized, and improve at your own pace.
